exp: Abate (verb): to become less intense, diminish, or decrease in amount or intensity. Example: The storm began to abate after several hours of heavy rain and strong winds. Let us first understand the given options: Lessen (verb): To make smaller in amount, degree, or intensity; to reduce or decrease. Example: Taking breaks throughout the day can help lessen the stress of a busy schedule. Sustain (verb):To maintain, support, or keep going; to endure or withstand. Example: The company's strong financial foundation allowed it to sustain its operations during difficult times. Increase (verb):To make greater in size, amount, or degree; to grow or become more. Example: The company decided to increase its production to meet higher demand. Subside (verb): To become less intense, violent, or active; to abate or settle down after reaching a peak. Example: The pain in his ankle began to subside after he applied ice and rested. Hence, “Increase” is the most appropriate antonym for the word “Abate”.

q: Identify the word in the given sentence that is the ANTONYM of the word 'frugal'. At present, people are paying extravagant prices for houses because they have to have accommodation.
subject: english
exp: Frugal - spending money carefully and avoiding waste. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Prices: the amount of money required to buy something. Accommodation: a place to live or stay. Present: existing or happening now. Extravagant: spending too much or being wasteful. Since "Extravagant" means spending excessively, it is the correct antonym of "Frugal." Hence, "Extravagant" is the correct answer.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Divergence
subject: english
exp: Correct Answer: Option A (Convergence) Divergence: The process of separating or moving apart. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Convergence: The process of coming together or uniting. Existence: The state of being real or present. Resilience: The ability to recover quickly from difficulties. Transience: The state of being temporary or short-lived. Since "Convergence" means coming together, which is the opposite of "Divergence," it is the correct antonym.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Most of the things in life are transient.
subject: english
exp: Let us first understand the given options: Transient (adjective): Lasting for a short time; temporary or fleeting. Example: The beauty of the transient cherry blossoms in spring is a sight to behold. Boring (adjective): Lacking interest or excitement; dull or unstimulating. Example: The lecture was so boring that some attendees fell asleep. Permanent (adjective): Lasting or intended to last indefinitely; not temporary or changeable. Example: The ink used in the document is permanent and cannot be easily removed. Toxic (adjective): Containing harmful or poisonous substances; capable of causing harm to living organisms. Example: Exposure to toxic chemicals can have severe health consequences. Fleeting (adjective): Passing quickly; lasting for a very short time. Example: The fleeting moment of a shooting star is a rare and magical sight. Hence, “Permanent” is the correct answer.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The love between them is blooming.
subject: english
exp: Blooming: thriving or flourishing, especially in a positive way. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Flourishing: thriving or growing well. Sprouting: starting to grow or develop. Growing: increasing in size or developing. Fading: gradually becoming weaker or less strong. Since "Fading" means becoming weaker or less intense, which is the opposite of "Blooming", it is the correct antonym. Hence, "Fading" is the correct answer.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. He was riding his bike on a bumpy surface.
subject: english
exp: Let us first understand the given options: Bumpy (adjective): Having a surface that is uneven or has irregularities. Example: The bumpy road made the journey uncomfortable. Jerky (adjective): Characterized by abrupt, sudden movements. Example: The jerky motion of the train made it difficult to sleep. Uneven (adjective): Not level or smooth; irregular. Example: The ground was uneven, making it challenging to walk. Choppy (adjective): Having short, irregular waves or a rough, turbulent surface. Example: The choppy waters made sailing difficult for the small boat. Steady (adjective): Firmly fixed, supported, or balanced; constant and regular. Example: The steady progress of the project reassured the team. Hence, “Steady” is the correct answer.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. She will probably come tonight for a party.
subject: english
exp: Correct Answer: Option A (Definitely) Probably: Likely to happen or be true but not certain. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Definitely: Certainly; without any doubt. Perhaps: Possibly; expressing uncertainty. Apparently: As far as one knows or can see. Possibly: Capable of happening, but not certain. Since "Definitely" means with certainty and no doubt, it is the correct antonym.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. I must have a chance to rectify my error.
subject: english
exp: The Correct Answer: Option A — Worsen Rectify: To correct or make right; to put something right that was wrong. (हिंदी अर्थ: सही करना या ठीक करना; किसी गलती को दूर करना।) Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Option A: Worsen – To make or become worse; to deteriorate. (हिंदी अर्थ: और खराब करना या होना; बिगड़ना।) Option B: Alter – To change or make different; to modify. (हिंदी अर्थ: बदलना या अलग करना; संशोधित करना।) Option C: Reason – The cause, explanation, or justification for an action or event; to think logically. (हिंदी अर्थ: किसी क्रिया या घटना का कारण, स्पष्टीीकरण, या औचित्य; तर्क करना।) Option D: Modify – To make partial or minor changes to something; to alter. (हिंदी अर्थ: किसी चीज़ में आंशिक या मामूली बदलाव करना; बदलना।) Since Worsen means to make something worse, it is the correct antonym of Rectify (which means to correct or make right). Additional Notes Wo rd Meanin g (अर्थ) Synonyms (पर्यायवाची) Antonym s (विलोम) Rec tify सुधारना / ठीक करना correct, remedy, fix worsen, damage, ruin Wo rse n बिगाड़ना / खराब करना aggravate, exacerbate, deteriorate rectify, improve, better Alt er बदलना / परिवर्तन करना change, modify, adjust keep, preserve, maintain Rea son कारण / तर्क cause, logic, justification Mo dify संशोधित करना / बदलना alter, change, adjust keep, preserve, retain

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Spurious
subject: english
exp: "Spurious" means something fake, false, or not genuine. Meanings of the other options: Fake: "Fake" is a synonym of "spurious" in this context. Both words refer to something that is not genuine or authentic. Stolen: "Stolen" refers to something that has been unlawfully taken or removed without the owner's consent. Splendid: "Splendid" means magnificent, excellent, or impressive. Authentic: If something is authentic, it is real, true, or what people say it is Hence, the correct option is (D).

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Innate
subject: english
exp: Innate - existing naturally from birth; inherent. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Learned: acquired through study or experience. Aquatic: related to water. Natural: existing in nature, not made or influenced by humans. Replete: filled or well-supplied with something. Since "Learned" means "acquired through experience or education," which is the opposite of "Innate" (natural or inborn), it is the correct antonym. Hence, "Learned" is the correct answer. ✅

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The truck was carrying a heavy load.
subject: english
exp: Load: the amount of weight carried by something. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Big: of considerable size or extent. Massive: very large or heavy. Light: having little weight; not heavy. Large: of great size or capacity. Since "Light" means having little weight and is the opposite of "Load", it is the correct antonym. Hence, "Light" is the correct answer.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. Henry is so servile that other people take advantage of him.
subject: english
exp: Servile: Excessively obedient and submissive, often in a way that is demeaning. Let us first understand the given options: Arrogant: Having an exaggerated sense of self-importance, often looking down on others. Sheepish: Showing embarrassment or modesty, often with a shy or awkward demeanor. Bickering: Engaging in petty arguments, often over trivial matters. Cunning: Having skill in achieving goals through deceit or trickery. Hence, “Arrogant” is the most appropriate antonym for the word “Servile”.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The panel picked the candidates randomly for the interview.
subject: english
exp: The word "randomly" in the sentence suggests that the panel selected candidates without a specific order or method. They chose candidates in an unplanned, haphazard manner, possibly without any particular criteria or organisation. The antonym "systematically" means doing something in a methodical, organised, and planned way. When the panel picks candidates systematically for the interview, it implies that they have a structured and well-thought-out process for selecting candidates. The meaning of the other options is as follows: "Casually" doesn't convey the opposite meaning, as it suggests a lack of seriousness rather than a lack of organisation. "Unwillingly" is unrelated to the concept of organisation or planning. "Incidentally" implies something happening by chance rather than with a deliberate plan. Hence, the correct option is (C).

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Lucy presented a lucid account of her achievements before the committee.
subject: english
exp: The most appropriate antonym of the underlined word "lucid" in the given sentence is ambiguous. "Lucid" means clear and easy to understand, so "ambiguous," which means unclear or having more than one possible interpretation, is the antonym that contrasts with "lucid" in this context. The meaning of the other options is as follows: "Orderly" means well-organised or arranged in a systematic manner. "Intelligible" means capable of being understood, often referring to language or communication. "Transparent" means easily seen through or understood, often suggesting clarity and openness. So, among the options provided, B) ambiguous is the most appropriate antonym for "lucid" in the context of the sentence. Hence, the correct option is (B).

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The reputed officer was removed from his post as he was having a clandestine affair.
subject: english
exp: The Correct Answer: Option B – Overt Clandestine - kept secret or done secretively, especially because illicit. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Surreptitious: kept secret, especially because it would not be approved of Overt: done or shown openly; not secret or hidden; opposite in meaning to clandestine. Amorous: showing, feeling, or relating to sexual desire Casual: relaxed and unconcerned Since Overt means open, public, and not secret, it is the correct antonym of Clandestine. q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM for the underlined word in the following sentence. That is a picture worth seeing but this is an absurd piece of art.
subject: english
exp: The correct answer is Option D - Realistic. Absurd means something that is illogical, ridiculous, or unreasonable. Option Analysis: Ordinary: Common, usual, or not special. Odd: Strange, unusual, or unexpected. Eerie: Mysterious, strange, and frightening. Realistic: Practical, sensible, and based on facts or reality.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. The lizard's light brown skin acts as camouflage in the desert sand.
subject: english
exp: Correct Answer: Option C ("reveal") Camouflage: The act of concealing or disguising something to blend in with its surroundings. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Clothed: Dressed or covered with clothes. Mask: To cover or hide something. Reveal: To make something known or visible. Vanishing: The act of disappearing. Since "reveal" means to make visible or expose, it is the correct antonym.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word to fill in the blank. While most friends persuaded him to choose the career, there were a few who _________ him from doing so.
subject: english
exp: Let's understand the meanings of the given words - Persuaded (मनाया) - Convinced someone to do or believe something through reasoning or argument. Evaded (टाल दिया) - Avoided or escaped from a situation, often by cleverness or deceit. Bombarded (बमबारी की) - Attacked continuously with bombs, shells, or other missiles; overwhelmed with questions or information. Dissuaded (रोक दिया) - Convinced someone not to do something through reasoning or persuasion. Situated (स्थित) - Located or placed in a particular position or place. Hence, the correct answer is "Dissuaded."

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. This is such a rowdy classroom.
subject: english
exp: Correct Answer: Option A (Compliant) Rowdy: Noisy and disorderly. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Compliant: Willing to conform or obey. Chattering: Talking rapidly and continuously, often in an annoying way. Disorderly: Not organized or lacking in order; chaotic. Noisy: Making a lot of sound. Since "Compliant" means obedient or willing to conform, which is the opposite of "Rowdy," it is the correct antonym.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The village fair was cancelled due to incessant rains.
subject: english
exp: Incessant: Continuing without interruption; never-ending. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Insistent: Demanding something persistently. Daily: Occurring every day; regular. Sporadic: Occurring at irregular intervals; scattered or infrequent. Continual: Repeated regularly or without interruption, but may have brief pauses. Since "Sporadic" means occurring at irregular intervals or infrequently, it is the correct antonym of "Incessant." Hence, "Sporadic" is the correct answer.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Memory
subject: english
exp: Memory: the ability to remember or recall past events, while oblivion means the state of being unaware or forgetful of past events. Example: My memory of that vacation is still very vivid. Let us first understand the given options: Suppress: to restrain or stop something. Example - The government tried to suppress the information. Oblivion: the state of being completely forgotten or unknown. Example - The once-famous singer had fallen into oblivion. Retrospection: the act of looking back or reflecting on the past. Example - In retrospection, she realized her mistakes. Impart: to transmit or convey information or knowledge. Example - The teacher wanted to impart wisdom to her students. Hence, “Oblivion” is the most appropriate antonym for the word “Memory”.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Aunt May thought that Uncle Ken's job as a manager at Firpo's hotel in Shimla was a stable one.
subject: english
exp: The Correct Answer: Option D – Mutable | | SSC Stable: Not likely to change or fail; firmly established; steady and reliable. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Lucrative: Producing a great deal of profit; profitable. Permanent: Lasting or intended to last or remain unchanged indefinitely; enduring. Durable: Able to withstand wear, pressure, or damage; hard-wearing. Mutable: Liable to change; changeable or unstable. Since Mutable means subject to change or not constant, it is the correct antonym of Stable. q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. He was quite violent in his behaviour.
subject: english
exp: The correct answer is Option C - Gentle. Violent: Using or involving physical force intended to hurt or damage. Option Analysis: Crazy: Mentally unstable or wild. Aggressive: Ready to attack or confront; forceful. Gentle: Mild, kind, and not harsh or violent (Opposite of violent). Savage: Fierce, cruel, and violent.

q: Choose the ANTONYM of the word 'disenfranchise' in the given sentence. The parliament of Tonoco authorised the king to take over the reins of the government and disable the power of the council of ministers.
subject: english
exp: Let us first understand the given options: Disenfranchise (verb): Deprive someone of their rights, especially the right to vote. Example: The discriminatory laws sought to disenfranchise certain groups, limiting their participation in elections. Authorised (adjective/verb): Having official approval or permission; to give official approval or power. Example: Only authorized personnel are allowed to access the restricted area. Powers (noun/verb): The ability or capacity to do something; the possession of control or influence. Example: The president has executive powers to make decisions and implement policies. Disabled (adjective): Having a condition that restricts one's abilities; impaired physically or mentally. Example: The organization works to create inclusive spaces for people with disabilities. Reins (noun): Straps or lines used to control a horse or other animal; symbolic of control or authority. Example: The coach handed over the reins of the team to the new captain. Hence, “Authorised” is the correct answer.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Seldom had he been so joyous in other's happiness.
subject: english
exp: Let us understand the meanings of the given words: The word, 'Seldom' means 'rarely', not often. It is the opposite of the word 'frequently' which means 'regularly or habitually' or 'often'. The word 'Hardly' means 'almost not'; 'barely'. The word 'Rarely' is the synonym of 'seldom'. The word 'Occasionally' means 'at infrequent or irregular intervals; now and then. Hence, "Frequently" is the most appropriate antonym for the word "Seldom".

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word in the following sentence. Traitor People should vote for honest and loyal leaders, not for betrayers and defenders.
subject: english
exp: The Correct Answer: Option D – Loyal Traitor: A person who betrays a friend, country, principle, etc.; someone who is disloyal. (हिंदी अर्थ: एक व्यक्ति जो किसी मित्र, देश, सिद्धांांत आदि के साथ विश्वाासघात करता है; कोई जो अविश्वसनीय है।) Let's learn the meaning of the options in the context of the question: Option A: Honest – Truthful and straightforward; not deceptive or fraudulent. (हिंदी अर्थ: सच्चा और सीधा; भ्राामक या धोखेबाज नहीं।) Option B: Defender – A person who defends someone or something; a protector. (हिंदी अर्थ: एक व्यक्ति जो किसी की या किसी चीज की रक्षाा करता है; एक रक्षक।) Option C: Betrayer – A person who betrays someone or something; a traitor. (हिंदी अर्थ: एक व्यक्ति जो किसी का या किसी चीज का विश्वाासघात करता है; एक देशद्रोोही।) Option D: Loyal - Giving or showing firm and constant support or allegiance to a person or institution; faithful. (हिंदी अर्थ: किसी व्यक्ति या संस्था को दृढ़ और स्थिर समर्थन या निष्ठाा देना या दिखाना; वफादार।) | | SSC Since Loyal means being faithful and showing constant support, it is the correct antonym of Traitor. q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Theirs is a clandestine affair.
subject: english
exp: The most appropriate antonym of the underlined word "clandestine" is: Known. "Clandestine" means secret or hidden, so "known" (meaning not secret and openly recognised) is its opposite. The meaning of the other options is as follows: Private: While "private" can sometimes be used as a synonym for "clandestine," in this context, "private" still implies a degree of secrecy, but not necessarily in a negative or secretive manner. Sneaky: "Sneaky" is a synonym of "clandestine," meaning behaving in a secretive, furtive, or sly way. It is not an antonym. Illicit: It refers to something that is forbidden by law, rules, or custom, often implying secrecy or wrongdoing. It is not an antonym. So, the correct antonym is A. Known, which indicates that something is not secret and is openly recognised, in contrast to "clandestine," which means hidden or secret.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The crowd gave an exultant cheer to the football team when they were exiting the airport.
subject: english
exp: The Correct Answer: Option B - Disconsolate Exultant - Feeling or expressing great joy and triumph. Option Analysis: Option A: Rejoicing - Expressing happiness and celebration. Option B: Disconsolate - Very unhappy or unable to be comforted (opposite of exultant). Option C: Jubilant - Feeling or expressing great joy and triumph. Option D: Elated - Extremely happy and excited.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined words in the given sentence. Developed countries are at a disadvantage in having access to modern science and technologies.
subject: english
exp: Correct Answer: Option B (Privileged) Disadvantage: An unfavorable condition or situation that reduces the chances of success. Let's learn the meaning of the given options: Powered: Supplied with energy or force. Privileged: Having special rights, advantages, or benefits. Useless: Not serving any purpose or function. Unhappy: Not feeling joy or satisfaction. Since "Privileged" means having advantages, which is the opposite of "Disadvantage," it is the correct antonym.

q: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The colossal building stood amidst the ruins bearing signs of Victorian architecture.
subject: english
exp: The Correct Answer: Option D – Tiny Colossal: Extremely large or great; gigantic. Let’s learn the meaning of the given options: Enormous: very large in size, quantity, or extent; huge. Since Tiny means very small, it is the correct antonym of Colossal. q: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Lachrymose
subject: english
exp: The Correct Answer: Option D — Jovial LACHRYMOSE: inclined to cry easily; showing sorrow or sadness (अश्रुपूर्ण / रोने वाला) Let’s learn the meaning of the given options: Option A: Tearful – full of tears; ready to cry (आँसू भरा) Option B: Mournful – feeling or showing deep sadness (शोकाकुल) Option C: Weepy – prone to crying (रोने वाला) Option D: Jovial – cheerful and full of high spirits (हँसमुख) Since lachrymose describes a sorrowful, tearful state, its most appropriate antonym is jovial, which conveys cheerfulness and happiness. q: Select the antonym of the given word. SCARCE
subject: english
exp: The word “scarce” refers to something that is in short supply or not readily available. To find its antonym, we need a word that suggests abundance or ample availability. Let’s evaluate the options: Scanty (A): This means small or insufficient in quantity or amount. It is similar in meaning to “scarce” and therefore not an antonym. Few (B): This implies a small number of something. Like “scanty,” it indicates a limited quantity, so it is not the antonym of “scarce.” Seldom (C): This refers to something that happens infrequently or rarely. It is related to frequency rather than quantity, so it does not serve as an antonym to “scarce.” Plentiful (D): This means existing in or yielding great quantities; more than enough. It is the opposite of “scarce,” as it indicates that there is a large amount or supply of something. Therefore, the correct antonym for “scarce” is “plentiful.”

q: Select the antonym of the given word. EXPANSION
subject: english
exp: The word “expansion” refers to the process of becoming larger or more extensive. To find its antonym, we need a word that means the opposite, which would be a process of becoming smaller or less extensive. Here’s a breakdown of the options: Inflation (A): This generally refers to the increase in prices and fall in the purchasing value of money. It can also mean the action of inflating something or the condition of being inflated, but it does not necessarily imply a reduction in size or extent. Augmentation (B): This means the action or process of making or becoming greater in size or amount. It is a synonym for expansion, not an antonym. Extension (C): This refers to a part that is added to something to enlarge or prolong it; a continuation. Like augmentation, it is also a synonym for expansion. Compression (D): This is the action of compressing or being compressed. It means making something smaller or more compact, which is the opposite of expansion. Therefore, the correct antonym for “expansion” is “compression”.

q: A series is given, with one word missing. Choose the correct alternative from the given ones that will complete the series as per dictionary. ?, Lifelike, Lifeline, Lifelong
subject: english
exp: Series given (dictionary order): ? , Lifelike, Lifeline, Lifelong All words begin with “life”. So we compare letters after life (i.e., start from the 5th letter) to decide alphabetical order. Write the relevant tails for each option and the given words: Lifelike → lifel i ke (5th = l, 6th = i) Lifeline → lifel i ne (5th = l, 6th = i, 7th = n) Lifelong → lifel o ng (5th = l, 6th = o) Now the options: A. Lifeless → lifel e ss (5th = l, 6th = e) B. Lifespan → life s pan (5th = s) C. Lifelost → lifel o st (5th = l, 6th = o) D. Lifework → life w ork (5th = w) Step-by-step comparison All candidates that start lifel- (A, C, and the three given words) are grouped together because their 5th letter is l. Options B and D start life + s/w, so they come later (since s and w are alphabetically after l). Within the lifel- group, compare the 6th letter: Lifeless (A): 6th = e Lifelike / Lifeline: 6th = i Lifelost (C) / Lifelong: 6th = o Alphabetical order: e < i < o. So Lifeless comes before Lifelike/Lifeline, and those come before Lifelost/Lifelong. Between Lifelike and Lifeline they share same 6th letter i, so we compare the 7th letter: k (in Lifelike) and n (in Lifeline). Since k < n, Lifelike comes before Lifeline — matching the given order. Conclusion: The word that correctly appears immediately before Lifelike in dictionary order is Lifeless. Hence, Lifeless is the correct answer.

q: Arrange the following words in a logical and meaningful order. 1. Wrist 2. Nails 3. Shoulder 4. Elbow 5. Palm 6. Fingers

Shoulder: This is the starting point as it is the uppermost part of the arm. 2. Elbow: The next part down the arm from the shoulder. 3. Wrist: Moving further down from the elbow. 4. Palm: The area of the hand that comes after the wrist. 5. Fingers: The parts extending from the palm. 6. Nails: These are located at the end of the fingers. Thus, the sequence 3 (Shoulder), 4 (Elbow), 1 (Wrist), 5 (Palm), 6 (Fingers), 2 (Nails) logically represents the progression from the shoulder down to the nails. Hence, 3, 4, 1, 5, 6, 2 is the correct answer.

q: Select the correct option that indicates the arrangement of the given words in the order in which they appear in an English dictionary. 1. Tranquil 2. Transit 3. Transfer 4. Transient 5. Training
subject: english
exp: Step 1 — Find the earliest split All words start with T-r-a. Check the 4th letter: Training (5): 4th = i → T-r-a-i... All others (1,2,3,4): 4th = n → T-r-a-n... Since i < n, Training (5) comes first. Step 2 — Compare the remaining “Tran-” words Now compare the 5th letter of the remaining four: Tranquil (1): 5th = q → T-r-a-n-q... Transit (2): 5th = s → T-r-a-n-s... **Transfer (3):**5th = s → T-r-a-n-s... **Transient (4):**5th = s → T-r-a-n-s... Alphabetically q < s, so Tranquil (1) comes next. Step 3 — Order the “Trans-” group (2,3,4) All three start T-r-a-n-s; compare the 6th letter: Transfer (3): 6th = f → ...-f... Transit (2): 6th = i → ...-i... Transient (4): 6th = i → ...-i... Since f < i, Transfer (3) comes before the other two. Between Transit (2) and Transient (4) (both have 6th = i), compare the 7th letter: Transit: 7th = t Transient:7th = e Since e < t, Transient (4) comes before Transit (2). So the Trans-group order is: Transfer (3) → Transient (4) → Transit (2). Step 4 — Final combined order Putting all parts together (earliest → latest): Training (5) → Tranquil (1) → Transfer (3) → Transient (4) → Transit (2) Numeric sequence: 5, 1, 3, 4, 2 Hence, 5, 1 ,3, 4, 2 is the correct answer.

q: After arranging the given words according to dictionary order, which word will come at ‘Fourth’ position? 1. Unseen 2. Unseam 3. Unsafe 4. Unsound 5. Unsay

Move to the 4th letter to decide order: Unsafe → 4th = a Unsay → 4th = a Unseam → 4th = e Unseen → 4th = e Unsound→ 4th = o Alphabetical order of these 4th letters: a < e < o. So all Unsa- words come first, then Unse-, then Unso-. Step 2 — Order within Unsa- group (Unsafe, Unsay) Compare the 5th letters: Unsafe: 5th = f Unsay: 5th = y Since f < y, Unsafe comes before Unsay. So Unsa-group order: Unsafe (3), Unsay (5). Step 3 — Order within Unse- group (Unseam, Unseen) Compare the 5th letters: Unseam: 5th = a Unseen: 5th = e Since a < e, Unseam comes before Unseen. So Unse-group order: Unseam (2), Unseen (1). Step 4 — Combine groups Final dictionary order (earliest → latest): Unsafe (3) → Unsay (5) → Unseam (2) → Unseen (1) → Unsound (4) The 4th word in this order is Unseen (1). Hence, 'Unseen' is the correct answer.

q: Select the option that represents the correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary. 1- Laudanum 2- Lattice 3- Laudation 4- Latterly 5- Laudable
subject: english
exp: Step 1 — Compare from the start All words begin with “La”. Move to the 3rd letter to decide order: Lattice (2): 3rd = t → L-a-t... Latterly (4): 3rd = t → L-a-t... Laudanum (1): 3rd = u → L-a-u... Laudation (3): 3rd = u → L-a-u... Laudable (5): 3rd = u → L-a-u... Since t < u, all “Lat-” words come before the “Lau-” words. So the two Lat- words (2 and 4) will be first, followed by the three Lau- words (1, 3, 5) in some order. Step 2 — Order the “Lat-” group (2 and 4) Both start l-a-t-t. Compare the 5th letter: Lattice (2): 5th = i → l-a-t-t-i... Latterly (4): 5th = e → l-a-t-t-e... Since e < i, Latterly (4) comes before Lattice (2). So Lat-group order: 4, 2. Step 3 — Order the “Lau-” group (1, 3, 5) All three begin l-a-u-d-a (the first five letters are identical). Move to the 6th letter: Laudable (5): 6th = b → l-a-u-d-a-b... Laudanum (1): 6th = n → l-a-u-d-a-n... Laudation (3): 6th = t → l-a-u-d-a-t... Alphabetical order: b < n < t, so the Lau-group order is Laudable (5) → Laudanum (1) → Laudation (3) i.e. 5, 1, 3. Step 4 — Combine both groups Putting the Lat- group first, then the Lau- group: 4, 2, 5, 1, 3 Hence , ” 4 , 2 , 5 , 1 , 3 ” is the correct answer.

q: Select the option that represents the correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary. 1. Banished 2. Bandage 3. Bannister 4. Bankrupt 5. Bandwagon 6. Banquet
subject: english
exp: Step 1 — Compare from the start All words begin with “ban” (B–A–N). Move to the 4th letter to decide order: 4th letters: Bandage (2): 4th = d → b-a-n-d... Bandwagon (5): 4th = d → b-a-n-d... Banished (1): 4th = i → b-a-n-i... Bankrupt (4): 4th = k → b-a-n-k... Bannister (3): 4th = n → b-a-n-n... Banquet (6): 4th = q → b-a-n-q... Alphabetical order of these 4th letters: d < i < k < n < q. So all ban-d words come first, then ban-i, ban- k, ban-n, ban-q. Step 2 — Order the “band-” group (2 and 5) Both start band. Compare the 5th letter: Bandage (2): 5th = a → b-a-n-d-a... **Bandwagon (5):**5th = w → b-a-n-d-w... Since a < w, Bandage (2) comes before Bandwagon (5). So the band-group order: 2 → 5 Step 3 — Combine remaining words in order of their 4th letters After the band-group, the next words in order of their 4th letters are: Banished (1) (4th = i) Bankrupt (4) (4th = k) Bannister (3) (4th = n) Banquet (6) (4th = q) So that sub-order is: 1 → 4 → 3 → 6 Step 4 — Final dictionary order (earliest → latest) Combine everything: Bandage (2), Bandwagon (5), Banished (1), Bankrupt (4), Bannister (3), Banquet (6) Numeric sequence: 2, 5, 1, 4, 3, 6 Hence, 2, 5, 1, 4, 3, 6 is the correct answer.

q: Select the option that represents the correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary. 1.Decline 2.Decouple 3.Decrease 4.Decorate 5.Decorum 6.Decode
subject: english
exp: Here, in this question we have to arrange the given words according to the English dictionary order. The correct dictionary order is as:- 1. Decline 6. Decode 4. Decorate 5. Decorum 2. Decouple 3. Decrease Hence, "1,6,4,5,2,3" is the correct answer.

q: Select the option that represents the correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary. 1. Refinery 2. Reflect 3. Reference 4. Refugee 5. Refillable 6. Reformist
subject: english
exp: Here in this question some words are given. We have to find the correct sequence of these words in which they occur according to the English alphabetical series like-A,B,C…and so on. Given - 1. Refinery 2. Reflect 3. Reference 4. Refugee 5. Refillable 6. Reformist Firstly we have to see the starting letters of the given words. The first, second and third letter of all the words are the same. Fourth letter of all the words- I,L,E,U,I,O By arranging the fourth letters in ascending order according to the English alphabetical series- E, I, I, L,O,U Reference- Refillable - Refinery- Reflect - Reformist - Refugee Fifth letters of the words Refillable and Refinery- L,N By arranging the fifth letter in ascending order according to the English alphabetical series. L,N So it is clear that the sequence of the words should be- Reference- Refillable - Refinery- Reflect - Reformist - Refugee So, it is clear that the sequence of the words should be- 3,5,1,2,6,4 Hence, “3,5,1,2,6,4” is the correct answer.

q: Right to Information Act ______ mandates a timely response to citizen requests for government information.
subject: polity
exp: The Right to Information (RTI) Act was enacted by the Parliament of India in 2005. It gives citizens the legal right to access information held by public authorities, promoting transparency and accountability in governance. The Act mandates that government officials must respond in a time-bound manner (usually within 30 days) to RTI applications filed by citizens. It has been a key tool in exposing corruption, improving public services, and empowering citizens to participate in democracy. Hence, the correct answer is "2005".

q: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution are related to citizenship?
subject: polity
exp: Part II of the constitution discuss the matters of citizenship of India under article 5-11. This part-II of constitution broadly classified the 4 categories only. The person who falls under categories might be the citizen of India from 26-01- 1950. 1. Person domicile in India 2. Persons who migrated from Pakistan 3. Persons who migrated to Pakistan 4. Person who is living abroad. Articles 15 to 21 of the Indian Constitution deal with fundamental rights. Articles 2 to 4 deal with the admission or establishment of new states and alteration of areas, boundaries, or names of existing states. Articles 25 to 31 pertain to fundamental rights related to freedom of religion, cultural and educational rights, and the right to property. Hence the correct answer is "Articles 5 to 11".

q: __________ changes each time it is installed to avoid detection by antivirus software.
subject: computer
exp: Some viruses are designed to change their code each time they spread. This change helps them avoid detection by antivirus software. Such viruses encrypt or modify their structure while keeping the same function. These characteristics describe a polymorphic virus. Hence, the correct answer is "Polymorphic virus".

q: A__________ is an agreement between the communicating parties on how communication is to proceed.
subject: computer
exp: In communication systems, certain rules must be followed for data exchange. These rules define how data is formatted, transmitted, and received. An agreement on communication procedures between parties is called a protocol. Other options do not define communication rules. Hence, the correct answer is "Protocol".

q: Which of the following is an application protocol?
subject: computer
exp: The Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) is an application protocol used for distributed, collaborative, and hypermedia information systems, serving as the foundation of data communication for the World Wide Web. HTTP (Hypertext Transfer Protocol) – Correct. It operates at the Application Layer (Layer 7 of the OSI model) and is used to load webpages and transfer hypermedia documents. TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) – Incorrect. It is a Transport Layer protocol responsible for reliable, ordered, and error-checked delivery of data. IP (Internet Protocol) – Incorrect. It is a Network Layer (Internet Layer) protocol responsible for addressing and routing packets across the internet. UDP (User Datagram Protocol) – Incorrect. It is a Transport Layer protocol used for fast, connectionless, and lightweight communication, often for streaming or DNS. Hence, the correct answer is "HTTP".

q: The complete form of 'IC' in electronics is
subject: computer
exp: In electronics, IC refers to a miniaturized electronic circuit. It contains multiple electronic components like transistors and resistors on a single chip. ICs are used in computers, mobile phones, and other electronic devices. The full form of IC is Integrated Circuit. Hence, the correct answer is "Integrated circuit".

q: What is the full form of 'LAN'?
subject: computer
exp: A LAN stands for Local Area Network. It consists of a series of computers linked together to form a network in a specific location. The computers in a LAN connect via IP ethernet or Wi-Fi. A LAN is normally exclusive to an organization, such as a school, association or office. LAN offers the following benefits: Resource sharing The relationship between the client and the server Internet sharing Sharing applications and software Data security Easy and fast communication Computer ID Hence, the correct answer is "Local Area Network".

q: The web uses __________ to request and serve web pages and programs.
subject: computer
exp: HTML stands for Hypertext Markup Language. It is the standard markup language for creating web applications and web pages. With CSS known as Cascading Style Sheets and JavaScript, HTML forms a triad of cornerstone technologies for the WWW (World Wide Web). Web browsers collect HTML documents from a web server or from local storage and deliver them into multimedia web pages. Hence, the correct answer is “Hyper Text Markup Language”.

q: In which graphics, digital photos and scanned images are typically stored with extensions such as.bmp, .png, .jpg, .tif or.gif?
subject: computer
exp: In Bitmap graphics, digital photos and scanned images are typically stored with extensions such as .bmp, .png, .jpg, tif or gif. Bitmap is a format in which an image is shown and stored digitally. It comprises various dots which together form the image. Such dots are called pixels, therefore as the number of dots in an image increases the clarity of the image increases. Hence the correct answer is “ Bitmap.”

q: Netscape Navigator is a ____.
subject: computer
exp: Netscape Navigator is a web browser that was discontinued in 2002 due to increased use of Microsoft’s Internet Explorer. Netscape Navigator was the flagship product of Netscape Communication Corp. In the 1990s it was the dominant web browser in terms of usage share. Graphical user interface is an interface that allows users to interact with different electronic devices using icons and other visual indicators. Computer programming language: It allows the user to give instructions to a computer in a language the computer understands. A processor is a unified electronic circuit that performs the calculations that run a computer. Hence, the correct answer is “web browser”.

q: ___________ is not a storage device.
subject: computer
exp: Linux is not a storage device; it is the best-known and most-used open-source operating system. As an operating system, Linux sits underneath all of the other software on a computer. It receives requests from programs and relays these requests to the computer's hardware. Linux is known for its stability, security, and flexibility. Hence, the correct answer is "Linux."

q: In Microsoft Excel, the () function returns the largest value among the values passed as arguments.
subject: computer
exp: MAX in Microsoft Excel determines the highest cell value included in the argument. MAX returns the largest value from the numbers provided. MAX function ignores empty cells. Syntax for the MAX function in Excel is MAX(number 1, [Number 2,....number _n]). Hence, the correct answer is MAX.

q: In Microsoft Excel, the_________ () function is used to count the number of cells within a range that meet the given criteria.
subject: computer
exp: Excel COUNTIF function is used for counting cells within a given range that meet a certain criterion. COUNT function is used to get the number of entries in a number field that is in a range or array of numbers SUM function is used to add multiple numeric values within different cells. Hence, the correct answer is “COUNTIF”.

q: Which of the following is not among the three different forms of 'if statement?
subject: computer
exp: if…and is not among the three different forms of 'if statement. If statement in Python is a very powerful decision-making statement used to control the flow of execution of statements. It is a two-way decision-making statement used with a relational or logical expression. It can be used in five ways: If else Nested if If else if ladder Simple if Single Line if Hence, the correct answer is “if…and”.

q: In computer terminology, what is the full form of FTP?
subject: computer
exp: File Transfer Protocol (FTP) is a program for uploading and downloading files, typically those that are too big and would take too long to download via a regular email program as an attachment. The main features of FTP are as follows: It provides security for File Transfer. It also gives its users a well-maintained access control. It provides multiple access methods for file transfer. It hides the details of an individual's computer system. FTP was published in the 1970s as a response to comment during an MIT project, attributed to developer Abhay Bhushan. Hence, the correct answer is "File Transfer Protocol".

q: ______ optical data storage is a technology where data is stored in multiple layers in the optical disc.
subject: computer
exp: 3D optical data storage is a form of optical data storage in which any information can be recorded with three- dimensional resolution. The 3D disc consists of many layers of information. These layers of information are present at different depths in the media and each of these layers consists of a DVD-like spiral track. Hence, the correct answer is "3D".

q: What is the full form of ISDN?
subject: computer
exp: ISDN stands for Integrated Services Digital Network. It is a telephone network system that transmits data as well as voice over a digital line. It was designed to switch from landline technology to digital technology. These digital lines are commonly telephone lines & exchanges which are being established by the government. It was not possible before ISDN for ordinary telephone lines to provide fast transmission. ISDN was formally standardized in 1988 and became famous in the 1990s as a faster alternative to the dial-up connection for internet access. Hence, the correct answer is "Integrated Service Digital Network".

q: Which among the following is not an input device?
subject: computer
exp: A plotter is a type of computer printer specifically designed to produce high- quality, large-scale graphics such as blueprints, maps, and architectural drawings. It operates by moving a pen or other writing tool across the surface of a piece of paper to create images. Unlike input devices, which are used to input data into a computer system, plotters are used to produce hard copies or outputs of digital designs or drawings. Input devices include tools like keyboards, mice, scanners, and touchscreens, which are used to input data or commands into a computer system. Hence, the correct answer is "Plotter."

q: What is a bug in computer terminology?
subject: computer
exp: A software bug refers to an error in a computer program or system. These errors can lead to incorrect or unexpected results. Debugging is the process of identifying and fixing these bugs. Bugs can range from simple syntax errors to complex logic flaws. Fixing bugs ensures that the program functions correctly and as intended. Hence, the correct answer is "An error in the program."

q: In Microsoft Word, _______ allows us to move selected paragraphs to the left.
subject: computer
exp: Decrease indent is a feature in Microsoft Word. It allows users to shift selected paragraphs to the left. This adjustment is handy for organizing text or adjusting formatting. Increase Indent, Double Indent, and Single Indent perform different functions. Hence, the correct answer is "Decrease Indent."

q: ______ software are freely available and can be edited and customized by anyone.
subject: computer
exp: Open Source software is freely available for anyone to use, modify, and distribute. Its source code is accessible, allowing customization according to user needs. Examples include Linux, Apache, and Firefox. Open source promotes collaboration and transparency in software development. Hence, the correct answer is "Open Source".

q: 'Teach Text' is a text editor in which of the following operating systems?
subject: computer
exp: Teach text is a text editor of the Macintosh operating system. The operating system is defined as a computer program that organizes several other programs at the same time. Macintosh is an operating system developed by Apple Inc. to be installed and operated on the Apple Macintosh series of computers. It was introduced in 1984. Windows is an operating system developed and marketed by Microsoft. Google Chrome is a cross-platform web browser developed by Google. Hence the correct answer is “ Macintosh.”

q: Who created the first mechanical computer, which proved to be the prototype for future computers?
subject: computer
exp: The digital programmable computer was created by English polymath Charles Babbage. In 1822, Charles Babbage started developing the Difference Engine, the first automatic computing machine. Charles Babbage is famous for initiating the idea of a programmable computer and inventing the world's first mechanical computer. Hence, the correct answer is "Charles Babbage".

q: Which one among the following is the main system board of a computer?
subject: computer
exp: Mother Board is the main system board of a computer. It is the printed circuit board that holds all the components of a computer in one place. It is also known as a logic board or system board. It allows and holds connections between all peripheral devices of the computer. The main components of the Motherboard are the CPU, BIOS, RAM, USB etc. A keyboard is an input device and is the most basic way for the user to communicate with a computer. A Central Processing Unit or CPU is electronic machinery that carries out instructions from programs that allow a computer or other device to perform its tasks. A microchip -- also called a chip, computer chip or integrated circuit (IC) -- is a unit of integrated circuitry that is manufactured at a microscopic scale using a semiconductor material, such as silicon. Hence, the correct answer is "Motherboard".

q: ________ Disk Encryption is a technology (hardware or software) where data is encrypted before storage.
subject: computer
exp: Disk encryption is a technology that protects information by converting it into unreadable code that cannot be deciphered easily by unauthorized people. Whole disk encryption signifies that everything on disk is encrypted. Disk encryption uses disk encryption software or hardware to encrypt every bit of data that goes on a disk. It is used to prevent unauthorized access to the storage of data. Hence, the correct answer is “Whole”.

q: A________ is a collection of letters, digits, punctuation characters, and so on.
subject: computer
exp: In computer science, a string is a finite sequence of characters. These characters can include letters, numbers, punctuation marks, and symbols. Strings are commonly defined by their length, which is the number of characters they contain. They are often used to represent text in programming languages and data processing. Strings are versatile and fundamental data types in computer science. They enable manipulation, comparison, and processing of textual data. Hence, the correct answer is "String."

q: Notepad' is a text editor in which of the following operating systems?
subject: computer
exp: Notepad is a simple text editor for Microsoft Windows and a basic text editing program that allows computer users to create documents. Google Chrome is a free cross-platform web browser that was introduced for Microsoft Windows platforms in 2008, prior to later releases on Mac, Linux, and mobile devices. Mozilla Firefox is a fast and tidy open- source web browser. At its public launch in 2004, it was the first browser to challenge Microsoft Internet Explorer’s dominance. Mac was known as Macintosh until 1999. It is a family of personal computers designed and marketed by Apple Inc. Hence, the correct answer is “Windows”.

q: What is the full form of JPEG?
subject: computer
exp: JPEG: Joint Photographic Experts Group Joint Photographic Experts Group is the name of the group that created the JPEG standard often seen with the file extension. It is the method of compressing graphic images and is used for sending the images over the internet or for sorting images. The images are saved with a file extension name of .jpeg after they are compressed in jpeg. Hence, the correct answer is “Joint Photographic Experts Group”

q: Using one can resize, tag digital images, organize pictures into albums by drag and drop, can export the pictures for external use (by e-mail or print).
subject: computer
exp: Using Image Organizer, one can resize, tag digital images, organize pictures into albums by drag and drop, and export the pictures for external use (by e-mail or print). Data organization is the way to arrange the raw data in an understandable order with the help of data organizers. Media organization is an area of Business Administration that deals with organizing and supervising teams of media professionals, various mass communication channels and technologies, media and entertainment productions, and more. Hence, the correct answer is “Image Organizer”.

q: In Microsoft Excel, the () function returns the smallest value among the values passed as arguments.
subject: computer
exp: The Excel MIN function returns the smallest value from a supplied set of numeric values. The syntax of the function is: MIN( number1, [number2], ... ) where the number arguments are one or more numeric values, that you want to return the lower value of. The MIN function takes a column or two phrases as an argument and returns the smallest value. Hence, the correct answer is “MIN”.

q: Which of the following is the first working prototype of the Internet?
subject: computer
exp: The first working prototype of the internet, known as ARPANET (Advanced Research Projects Agency Network), was developed in the late 1960s by the United States Department of Defense's Advanced Research Projects Agency (ARPA). ARPANET aimed to create a network that could connect computers and allow for the sharing of information and resources. The first successful communication over ARPANET occurred on October 29, 1969, between two computers located at the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) and the Stanford Research Institute (SRI). This event marked the birth of the internet as we know it today. The development of ARPANET laid the foundation for modern Internet infrastructure and introduced many of the fundamental technologies and protocols that are still in use today. Hence, the correct answer is "ARPANET".

q: __________ is the founder of Facebook.
subject: computer
exp: Mark Zuckerberg is the principal founder of Facebook (now Meta Platforms), which he launched on February 4, 2004, from his dormitory room at Harvard University. He was assisted by his roommates and fellow students, including Eduardo Saverin, Dustin Moskovitz, and Chris Hughes. Mark Zuckerberg developed the initial site, then known as "TheFacebook," to help university students connect. He currently serves as the Chairman and CEO of Meta. Brian Acton is a computer programmer and entrepreneur, known as the co- founder of WhatsApp which is a widely used messaging app that allows users to send text messages, make voice and video calls, and share media files. Jimmy Wales is an American internet entrepreneur, best known as the co- founder of Wikipedia, the online encyclopedia that provides free and open content to users worldwide. Larry Page is an American computer scientist and entrepreneur, known as the co-founder of Google. Hence, the correct answer is "Mark Zuckerberg".

q: In MS-Excel 365 which of the following appears across the top of the screen and below the title bar and contains all the commands, formulas and features needed to create data- intense spreadsheets?
subject: computer
exp: In MS-Excel 365, the Ribbon appears across the top of the screen. It is located below the title bar, and contains all the commands, formulas, and features needed to create data- intense spreadsheets. It is organized into tabs (e.g., Home, Insert, Page Layout) that group related commands together. Hence, the correct answer is "Ribbon".

q: Which of the following is NOT an example of e- mail service provider? I. Outlook II. Gmail III. Yandex
subject: computer
exp: Outlook: This is a widely used e-mail service provider developed by Microsoft. It offers e-mail services along with other features like calendar, task manager, and contact manager. Gmail: This is Google's e-mail service provider. It is one of the most popular e- mail services in the world. Yandex: This is a Russian multinational corporation specializing in Internet- related products and services, including its e-mail service, Yandex.Mail. Hence, the correct answer is "All are e-mail service providers".

q: Which of the following is keyboard shortcut to reload the current page in Google search engine?
subject: computer
exp: Pressing F5 refreshes or reloads the current page in most web browsers, including Google Search. F1 typically opens the help menu. F3 is usually used to open a search function in many applications. F7 does not have a standard function related to page reloading. Hence, the correct answer is "F5".

q: ________ is a reading device that reads printed text by scanning it character by character.
subject: computer
exp: An Optical Character Reader (OCR) is a device that reads printed text by scanning it character by character. Track Ball is a pointing device that uses a ball to control the cursor on the screen. A digitizer is a device that converts analog signals or images into digital form, often used for graphics and drawings. Magnetic Ink Card Reader (MICR) is a device used to read magnetic ink characters, typically found on checks and bank documents. Hence, the correct answer is "Optical Character Reader (OCR)".

q: _______ viruses are embedded in a system's memory so it can be reactivated if the original virus is deleted.
subject: computer
exp: File viruses attach themselves to executable files and spread when the infected file is run. Resident viruses embed themselves in the system's memory. Even if the original virus file is deleted, the resident virus can be reactivated because it remains in memory. Polymorphic viruses can change their code or appearance to evade detection. A rootkit is a type of malware that is designed to enable unauthorized access to a computer while hiding its existence. Hence, the correct answer is "Resident".

q: A _______ is a networking device that filters network traffic while connecting multiple computers or communicating devices
subject: computer
exp: A switch is a networking device that connects multiple computers or other devices within a local area network (LAN) and filters traffic to ensure that data packets are only sent to the appropriate device. Repeater: This device regenerates and amplifies signals to extend the range of a network. Distributor: This term is less commonly used in networking. Router: While a router connects multiple networks and directs traffic between them, it is not primarily focused on filtering traffic within a single network segment like a switch. Hence, the correct answer is "Switch".

q: Which among the following statements is incorrect?
o: The right window of Windows Explorer is called Navigation Pane.
From the Navigation pane, you can view your computer's file and folder structure and access files and folders.
In the Navigation Pane, there is a Quick Access area.
Navigation Pane is to enable easier navigation through Windows Explorer.
subject: computer
exp: The Navigation Pane is located on the left side of the Windows Explorer window. The Navigation Pane allows users to easily browse and access the file and folder structure of their computer. The Quick Access area in the Navigation Pane displays frequently used folders and recently accessed files for easy access. The primary purpose of the Navigation Pane is to simplify navigation through files and folders within Windows Explorer, making it more user-friendly. Hence, the correct answer is "The right window of Windows Explorer is called Navigation Pane. ".

q: ________memory is sometimes used to increase the speed of processing by making current programs and data available to the CPU at a rapid rate.
subject: computer
exp: Backup refers to data that is saved for recovery purposes but does not directly relate to processing speed. Auxiliary memory typically refers to secondary storage (like hard drives) used for long-term data storage, which is slower than main memory. Main memory (or primary memory) is where the operating system, application programs, and currently processed data are kept for quick access. Cache memory is a small, high-speed storage location that temporarily holds frequently accessed data and instructions close to the CPU. This allows for quicker retrieval and processing, significantly increasing the overall speed of operations. Hence, the correct answer is "Cache".

q: ____holds address of the active memory location.
subject: computer
exp: The Memory Address Register (MAR) holds the address of the active memory location that the CPU is currently accessing or planning to access next. This register is used to fetch data from or send data to a specific memory location in the main memory. The Stack Pointer holds the address of the last program request in the stack. The program Counter holds the address of the next instruction to be executed. The instruction Register holds the current instruction being executed. Hence, the correct answer is "Memory Address Register".

q: Which of the following shortcut key is used to select all cells of the current row in MS-Excel 2010 worksheet?
subject: computer
exp: In MS Excel 2010, pressing Ctrl + Spacebar selects all the cells in the current column, not the row. Ctrl + Spacebar selects the entire column. Neither Shift + Page Down nor Shift + Page Up selects the entire row; they only extend the selection by a screen’s worth of cells. Hence, the correct answer is "Ctrl + Spacebar".

q: Which among the following is incorrect about Laser Printers?
o: Uses laser lights for producing dots to form characters on a page
Can print multiple copies at a time
subject: computer
exp: Laser printers use laser technology to project an image onto a drum, which is then transferred to paper using toner. They can only print one copy at a time. They are known for their high printing speeds. They generally have a wide range of font and size options available due to their advanced printing capabilities. Hence, the correct answer is "Can print multiple copies at a time".

q: The memory unit that communicates directly with the CPU is called ___________ memory.
subject: computer
exp: Main memory (also known as primary memory or RAM) is the memory that the CPU accesses directly for processing data and instructions. It is much faster than secondary storage (such as hard drives or SSDs). It is essential for the execution of programs and the operation of the computer system. Cache memory, although very fast, serves as a smaller, high-speed intermediary between the CPU and main memory, storing frequently accessed data to speed up operations. Hence, the correct answer is "main".

q: In Microsoft Word, what is the purpose of the 'Print Preview' feature?
o: To insert images and graphics into the document.
To format the font and style of the document.
To check the spelling and grammar of the document.
To view how the document will appear when printed.
subject: computer
exp: The Print Preview feature in Microsoft Word allows users to see how the document will appear when printed. It helps check the layout, margins, and overall appearance of the document. Users can ensure everything looks correct before finalizing the print. Hence, the correct answer is "To view how the document will appear when printed.".

q: Which of the following Internet protocol specifies how data is exchanged over the Internet and how it should be broken into IP packets?
subject: computer
exp: TCP is responsible for breaking down data into smaller packets and ensuring that they are sent and received in the correct order over the Internet. It establishes a connection between the sender and receiver and manages error checking and data flow control. PPP is used for direct connections between two network nodes. HTTP is an application-layer protocol used for transferring hypertext (web pages) on the Internet. SMTP is used for sending emails but, like HTTP. Hence, the correct answer is "TCP"

q: Shading option in MS-Word 365 is used for______
o: changing the color around the selected text
changing the color behind the selected text
changing the color of the selected text
making the text pop by highlighting it
subject: computer
exp: The shading option in MS Word 365 allows users to add a background color behind the selected text, enhancing its visibility. It does not change the color around the text but rather behind it. Changing the color of the text itself is done through the font color option, not shading. Hence, the correct answer is "changing the color behind the selected text".

q: _______cookies track you and expose your privacy.
subject: computer
exp: Third-party cookies are created by domains other than the one being visited. They are often used for tracking users across different websites for advertising and analytics purposes, which can lead to privacy concerns. Persistent cookies are stored on your device and can remain for a long time.They do not inherently track users across different sites. Zombi cookies are a type of cookie that can regenerate after being deleted. Session cookies are temporary and are deleted when the browser is closed. Hence, the correct answer is "Third-party".

q: Which among the following registers in Instruction Cycle holds the last instruction fetched?
subject: computer
exp: Memory Address Register (MAR): Holds the memory address of the data that needs to be accessed. Instruction Register (IR): Holds the last instruction fetched from memory before it is decoded and executed. Program Counter (PC): Holds the address of the next instruction to be executed, not the last instruction. Memory Buffer Register (MBR): Holds the data that is being transferred to or from memory. Hence, the correct answer is "Instruction Register (IR)".

q: ______displays a list of utility configure the computer system and install software and hardware.
subject: computer
exp: The Control Panel in Windows displays a list of utilities that allow users to configure the computer system, install software and hardware, and manage various settings related to the operating system and connected devices. It provides access to settings for system configuration, user accounts, network settings, and more. Hence, the correct answer is "Control Panel".

q: Which of the following font effects is not available in the MS-Word 2010 font dialog box?
subject: computer
exp: Superscript: This effect allows text to be displayed slightly above the baseline, often used for exponents or footnotes Small Cap: This effect converts lowercase letters to smaller capital letters, making text look uniform, which is often used for stylistic purposes in headings or titles. Double Strikethrough: This effect applies two horizontal lines through the text, used to indicate deletion or revision. Equalize Character Height: This is not a font effect available in MS Word 2010. While it sounds like a formatting option, MS Word does not have a feature specifically called "Equalize Character Height." Hence, the correct answer is " Equalize Character Height".

q: _________is the oldest type of Central Processing Unit (CPU)
subject: computer
exp: Triple Core is a type of CPU with three processing cores, which is more advanced than single-core processors. Single Core is the oldest type of CPU architecture, featuring only one core that handles all processing tasks. Single-core CPUs were standard before the introduction of multi-core processors. A Dual core type has two cores and is more advanced than single-core CPUs, allowing for better multitasking and performance. Quad Core has four cores, offering even greater performance and multitasking capabilities compared to single-core and dual-core processors. Hence, the correct answer is "Single Core".

q: Which of the following option can be pressed to create a new email message in Gmail?
subject: computer
exp: In Gmail, the "Compose" button is used to create a new email message. Settings refer to the Gmail settings menu, where preferences, account settings, and configure features like filters or themes are present. Outbound refers to outgoing emails, but Gmail doesn't have an option called "Outbound." Drafts is a folder that contains emails one has started but hasn't sent yet. Hence, the correct answer is "Compose".

q: _________ server acts as a set of componeats accessible to the software developer through an API defined by the platform itself
subject: computer
exp: An application server provides a set of components that software developers can access through an API defined by the platform itself. Mail typically refers to email servers that handle sending, receiving, and storing email messages. File Transfer Protocol (FTP)is a standard network protocol used for transferring files from one host to another over a TCP-based network. A database is a structured collection of data that can be accessed and managed. Hence, the correct answer is "Application".

q: Which one of the following folders is used to store deleted emails?
subject: computer
exp: The trash folder is where deleted emails are temporarily stored before being permanently removed from the account. This allows users to recover accidentally deleted emails if needed. Spam: This folder is used to store unsolicited or junk emails. Inbox: This is the primary folder where incoming emails are stored. Drafts: This folder is used to save emails that are not yet sent. Hence, the correct answer is "Trash".

q: Which among the following ports is also known as the Line Printer Port?
subject: computer
exp: The parallel port is also called the Line Printer (LPT) port, as it was originally developed to connect printers to computers. It transmits 8 bits of data simultaneously (in parallel), making it faster than earlier serial connections for printers. Typically found on older computers, it was labeled as LPT1, LPT2, etc. Other options: VGA (Video Graphics Array) → used for monitors. HDMI (High Definition Media Interface) → modern video/audio interface. Display Port → digital display interface for monitors/projectors. Hence, the correct answer is "Parallel".

q: In Gmail, what do you use when you receive a mail and want to send the mail in response of the same mail and to the original sender only?
subject: computer
exp: Forward: Used to send the email to someone else, not to the original sender. Reply All: Sends your response to the original sender and all other recipients. Reply: Sends a response only to the original sender of the email. Hence, the correct answer is "Reply".

q: A _________ is a contagious program or code that attaches itself to another piece of software, and then reproduces itself when that software is run.
subject: computer
exp: A virus is a contagious program or code that attaches itself to another piece of software and reproduces itself when that software is run. Examples are ILOVEYOU Virus, Melissa Virus, Conficker, and Sasser. Mydoom etc. The first computer virus is generally attributed to Bob Thomas, who created the Creeper virus in the early 1970s. Hence, the correct answer is "virus".

q: Which among the following keyboard shortcut can be used to open Windows Explorer?
subject: computer
exp: The keyboard shortcut Windows Key + E opens Windows Explorer (File Explorer) directly, allowing users to browse files and folders on their computer quickly. Windows Key + R: This shortcut opens the Run dialog box, which is used to run commands. Windows Key + X: This opens the Quick Link menu (or Power User menu). Windows Key + W: This shortcut opens Windows Ink Workspace. Hence, the correct answer is "Windows Key + E ".

q: Which among the following keyboard shortcuts is used to insert a hyperlink for the selected text in Microsoft Word?
subject: computer
exp: Ctrl + K is the keyboard shortcut used in Microsoft Word to insert a hyperlink for the selected text. Ctrl + M is used to increase the indent of a paragraph. Ctrl + H opens the "Find and Replace" dialog. Ctrl + Z is the shortcut for undoing the last action. Hence, the correct answer is "Ctrl+K".

q: ______records everything you type on your PC in order to obtain your log-in name, passwords and other sensitive information.
subject: computer
exp: Keyloggers are malicious software programs or devices that record every keystroke a user makes on their keyboard. This information, including login names, passwords, and other sensitive data, is then sent to a third party, typically without the user’s knowledge. Keyloggers are often used by cybercriminals to steal personal information, financial data, and credentials. Hence, the correct answer is "Keyloggers".

q: Which of the following is Math and trigonometric functions available in MS-Excel 365 ?
subject: computer
exp: Text function includes functions related to text manipulation, not specifically math or trigonometric functions. ABS function returns the absolute value of a number, which is a mathematical function. It is indeed categorized under math functions in Excel. Logical functions used for logical comparisons (like IF, AND, OR) and are not specifically math or trigonometric functions. Financial function are used for financial calculations (like PMT, FV, etc.) and are also not categorized under math or trigonometric functions. Hence, the correct answer is " ABS function".

q: Which among the following type of computer is also known as notebook?
subject: computer
exp: A notebook computer is a type of microcomputer. Microcomputers are small, personal computers designed for individual use, and this category includes desktops, laptops, and notebooks. Notebooks are specifically designed to be portable, lightweight, and compact, making them easy to carry around. Hence, the correct answer is "Micro Computer".

q: While ___________ , data transfers from the customers' machine to the server.
subject: computer
exp: Browsing involves viewing web pages, which mostly involves downloading data from the server to the customer's machine. Uploading allows transferring data from the customer's machine to the server. Forwarding usually refers to passing data from one place to another, but not necessarily involving a transfer to a server. Downloading allows transferring data from the server to the customer's machine. Hence, the correct answer is "uploading".

q: A ______ can either be malware or hardware. The main purpose of this malware is to record the keys pressed by a user on the keyboard.
subject: computer
exp: Keyloggers are a type of surveillance software that can be either malware or hardware designed to record the keystrokes made by a user on their keyboard. This data is often used for malicious purposes, such as stealing personal information, passwords, and sensitive data. They can operate in the background without the user's knowledge. Hence, the correct answer is "Keyloggers".

q: __was the first browser to display images inline with text instead of displaying images in a separate window.
subject: computer
exp: Mosaic was the first web browser to display images inline with text. It was released in 1993. It allowed users to view both text and images within the same window, enhancing the visual experience and contributing significantly to the popularity of the web. Hence, the correct answer is "Mosaic".

q: Which among the following statements is incorrect about Central Processing Unit?
o: Control Unit controls the order in which instructions enter and leave the processor and how the instructions are executed.
Arithmetic Logic Unit is responsible for taking the input, converting it into a signal, and storing it for further processing.
Arithmetic Logic Unit is also known as the mathematical brain of a computer.
Control Unit directs and manages operation sequences.
subject: computer
exp: The Control Unit does control the order of instruction execution. The Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) performs arithmetic and logical operations but does not handle input conversion or storage. T The ALU is often referred to as the "mathematical brain" of the computer because it performs mathematical calculations. The Control Unit does direct and manages the sequences of operations in the CPU. Hence, the correct answer is "Arithmetic Logic Unit is responsible for taking the input, converting it into a signal, and storing it for further processing.".

q: What is the primary purpose of RAM (Random Access Memory) in a computer?
o: Providing temporary storage for data that the CPU is currently processing
subject: computer
exp: The primary purpose of RAM (Random Access Memory) is to serve as a temporary storage area for data and instructions that the CPU (Central Processing Unit) needs while performing tasks. RAM allows quick access to data, making it ideal for handling active processes and running applications. RAM is volatile and loses data when powered off. Hence, the correct answer is "Providing temporary storage for data that the CPU is currently processing".

q: What is the keyboard shortcut for 'Copy' in most Windows applications?
subject: computer
exp: In most Windows applications, Ctrl + C is the keyboard shortcut for the "Copy" function, which copies the selected text or item to the clipboard. Ctrl + Z: Used to undo the last action. Ctrl + X: Used to cut the selected item (removing it and copying it to the clipboard). Ctrl + V: Used to paste the copied or cut item from the clipboard. Hence, the correct answer is "Ctrl + C.".

q: In File Explorer of Windows 10, the keyboard shortcut 'Num Lock + Asterisk sign (*)' is used to -_______ .
o: display all subfolders under the selected folder
open the properties dialog box for the selected item
display the contents of the selected folder
subject: computer
exp: In Windows 10's File Explorer, pressing Num Lock + Asterisk (*) will display all subfolders under the selected folder, expanding them for easier navigation. "Alt + Enter" open properties. "Enter" opens the folder contents. "Minus (-)" collapses the folder. Hence, the correct answer is "display all subfolders under the selected folder".

q: _________is also known as electronic disk.
subject: computer
exp: Solid-State Drive (SSD) is a storage device that uses flash memory to store data. SSDs have no moving parts, making them faster, more durable, and less prone to mechanical failure. This makes SSDs ideal for high- performance computing and data- intensive applications. Hence, the correct answer is "Solid-State Drive".

q: A / An _______ is an organisation that connects its subscriber's computer using modem to the Intemet.
subject: computer
exp: A / An Internet Service Provider (ISP) is an organization that connects its subscriber's computer using a modem to the Internet. It enables internet connectivity for individuals and businesses through various technologies like broadband, DSL, or fiber. It offers internet access through subscription plans, often including various speeds and data limits. Subscribers typically require a modem or router to connect to the ISP's network and access the internet. Hence, the correct answer is "Internet Service Provider".

q: Consider the following statements regarding line spacing of a paragraph in MS-Word 2010: P: Line spacing determines the amount of vertical space between lines of text in a paragraph. Q: Line spacing determines the amount of diagonal space between lines of text in a paragraph Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?
subject: computer
exp: Line spacing determines the amount of vertical space between lines of text in a paragraph. This is a fundamental aspect of formatting in MS Word, allowing users to adjust the readability and appearance of text. Line spacing does not determine the amount of diagonal space between lines of text. Hence, the correct answer is "Only P".

q: _______is a high-speed device used in CPU that is utilised to store data temporarily during processing.
subject: computer
exp: Registers are small, high-speed storage locations within the CPU. They temporarily hold data and instructions during processing. They are essential for the CPU's operation, allowing it to access and manipulate data quickly. They provide immediate access to the data the CPU needs to execute tasks efficiently. Hence, the correct answer is "Registers".

q: ______is a passage to connect two networks that may work on different networking models.
subject: computer
exp: A gateway is a device that acts as a "gate" between two networks, allowing them to communicate even if they use different networking models or protocols. The router directs data packets between networks but typically operates within the same networking model. Switch connects devices within the same network, operating at the data link layer. Hub is a basic device that connects multiple Ethernet devices, making them act as a single network segment but does not perform any routing or networking model conversion. Hence, the correct answer is "Gateway".

q: In MS-Word 365, Ctrl+E is the keyboard shortcut to_____
subject: computer
exp: In MS Word 365, the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + E is used to centre align the selected text. Justify the selected text: This is done with Ctrl + J.
